<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Otsurumaki Kofun]][[en:Ōtsurumaki Kofun]][[ja:大鶴巻古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Otsurumaki Kofun]][[en:Ōtsurumaki Kofun]][[ja:大鶴巻古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Otsurumaki Kofun]][[en:Ōtsurumaki Kofun]][[ja:大鶴巻古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17227935]] Wikidata name: Otsurumaki-kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōtsurumaki Kofun | native_name =大鶴巻古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Otsurumaki-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōtsurumaki Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōtsurumaki Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.294514|N|139.038722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Takasaki, Gunma|Takasaki, Gunma]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th to early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Ōtsurumaki Kofun'''|大鶴巻古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in what is now the city of [[:en:Takasaki, Gunma|Takasaki]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209740|title=大鶴巻古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is one of the largest ''kofun'' in Gunma Prefecture. It is estimated to have been built around the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century AD and is part of a group of 13 tumuli which were concentrated in the vicinity, forming the Kuragano Kofun Cluster. Within the Kuragano Kofun Cluster are a number of other very large keyhole-shaped tumuli, including the [[Sengenyama Kofun]] (171.5 meters long), which has a separate National Historic Site designation, and the Kotsurumaki Kofun (87.5 meters).
==Overview== The Ōtsurumaki Kofun is located on a plain to the southeast of the city center of Takasaki on the left bank of the Karasu River near the confluence of the Kabura River. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus has a total length of 123 meters, with a posterior circular portion in three tiers and an anterior rectangular portion two tiers, and is orientated to the southeast. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and both cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' and figurine ''haniwa'' (shaped as people and houses) have been recovered. In design, it closely resembles the Sakimisasagiyama Kofun in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}}, indicating a close connection with the Yamato kingdom. The tumulus has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] and its internal construction is unknown, but it is suspected to have a pit-type stone-lined [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus was surrounded by a moat, traces of which remain.
;Total length: 123 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 54 meters long x 51 meters wide x 6.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter x 10.5 meters high, 2-tier
The tumulus is about 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kuragano Station|en|Kuragano Station|WD=Q910582|lt=Kuragano Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Takasaki Line|en|Takasaki Line|WD=Q1346657|lt=Takasaki Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The adjacent Kotsurumaki Kofun, which is roughly contemporary in age and similar in construction but on a smaller scale, is not covered by the National Historic Site designation.

It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus has a total length of 123 meters, with a posterior circular portion in three tiers and an anterior rectangular portion two tiers, and is orientated to the southeast. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and both cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' and figurine ''haniwa'' (shaped as people and houses) have been recovered. In design, it closely resembles the Sakimisasagiyama Kofun in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}}, indicating a close connection with the Yamato kingdom. The tumulus has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] and its internal construction is unknown, but it is suspected to have a pit-type stone-lined [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus was surrounded by a moat, traces of which remain.
;Total length: 123 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 54 meters long x 51 meters wide x 6.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter x 10.5 meters high, 2-tier
The tumulus is about 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kuragano Station|en|Kuragano Station|WD=Q910582|lt=Kuragano Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Takasaki Line|en|Takasaki Line|WD=Q1346657|lt=Takasaki Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The adjacent Kotsurumaki Kofun, which is roughly contemporary in age and similar in construction but on a smaller scale, is not covered by the National Historic Site designation.
==Gallery== <gallery> Kotsurumaki-kofun zenkei.JPG|Kotsurumaki Kofun </gallery>

It is estimated to have been built around the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century AD and is part of a group of 13 tumuli which were concentrated in the vicinity, forming the Kuragano Kofun Cluster. Within the Kuragano Kofun Cluster are a number of other very large keyhole-shaped tumuli, including the [[Sengenyama Kofun]] (171.5 meters long), which has a separate National Historic Site designation, and the Kotsurumaki Kofun (87.5 meters).

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōtsurumaki Kofun | native_name =大鶴巻古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Otsurumaki-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōtsurumaki Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōtsurumaki Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.294514|N|139.038722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Takasaki, Gunma|Takasaki, Gunma]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th to early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Ōtsurumaki Kofun'''|大鶴巻古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in what is now the city of [[:en:Takasaki, Gunma|Takasaki]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209740|title=大鶴巻古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is one of the largest ''kofun'' in Gunma Prefecture. It is estimated to have been built around the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century AD and is part of a group of 13 tumuli which were concentrated in the vicinity, forming the Kuragano Kofun Cluster. Within the Kuragano Kofun Cluster are a number of other very large keyhole-shaped tumuli, including the [[Sengenyama Kofun]] (171.5 meters long), which has a separate National Historic Site designation, and the Kotsurumaki Kofun (87.5 meters).
==Overview== The Ōtsurumaki Kofun is located on a plain to the southeast of the city center of Takasaki on the left bank of the Karasu River near the confluence of the Kabura River. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus has a total length of 123 meters, with a posterior circular portion in three tiers and an anterior rectangular portion two tiers, and is orientated to the southeast. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and both cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' and figurine ''haniwa'' (shaped as people and houses) have been recovered. In design, it closely resembles the Sakimisasagiyama Kofun in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}}, indicating a close connection with the Yamato kingdom. The tumulus has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] and its internal construction is unknown, but it is suspected to have a pit-type stone-lined [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus was surrounded by a moat, traces of which remain.
;Total length: 123 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 54 meters long x 51 meters wide x 6.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter x 10.5 meters high, 2-tier
The tumulus is about 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kuragano Station|en|Kuragano Station|WD=Q910582|lt=Kuragano Station|1=Kuragano Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Takasaki Line|en|Takasaki Line|WD=Q1346657|lt=Takasaki Line|1=Takasaki Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The adjacent Kotsurumaki Kofun, which is roughly contemporary in age and similar in construction but on a smaller scale, is not covered by the National Historic Site designation.
==Gallery== <gallery> Kotsurumaki-kofun zenkei.JPG|Kotsurumaki Kofun </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|WD=Q16248234|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)}}
==References== {{reflist}}

The tumulus has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] and its internal construction is unknown, but it is suspected to have a pit-type stone-lined [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus was surrounded by a moat, traces of which remain.
;Total length: 123 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 54 meters long x 51 meters wide x 6.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter x 10.5 meters high, 2-tier
The tumulus is about 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kuragano Station|en|Kuragano Station|WD=Q910582|lt=Kuragano Station|1=Kuragano Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Takasaki Line|en|Takasaki Line|WD=Q1346657|lt=Takasaki Line|1=Takasaki Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The adjacent Kotsurumaki Kofun, which is roughly contemporary in age and similar in construction but on a smaller scale, is not covered by the National Historic Site designation.

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōtsurumaki Kofun | native_name =大鶴巻古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Otsurumaki-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ōtsurumaki Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōtsurumaki Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.294514|N|139.038722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Takasaki, Gunma|Takasaki, Gunma]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th to early 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Ōtsurumaki Kofun'''|大鶴巻古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in what is now the city of [[:en:Takasaki, Gunma|Takasaki]], {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/209740|title=大鶴巻古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is one of the largest ''kofun'' in Gunma Prefecture. It is estimated to have been built around the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century AD and is part of a group of 13 tumuli which were concentrated in the vicinity, forming the Kuragano Kofun Cluster. Within the Kuragano Kofun Cluster are a number of other very large keyhole-shaped tumuli, including the [[Sengenyama Kofun]] (171.5 meters long), which has a separate National Historic Site designation, and the Kotsurumaki Kofun (87.5 meters).
==Overview== The Ōtsurumaki Kofun is located on a plain to the southeast of the city center of Takasaki on the left bank of the Karasu River near the confluence of the Kabura River. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus has a total length of 123 meters, with a posterior circular portion in three tiers and an anterior rectangular portion two tiers, and is orientated to the southeast. The surface was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and both cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' and figurine ''haniwa'' (shaped as people and houses) have been recovered. In design, it closely resembles the Sakimisasagiyama Kofun in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}}, indicating a close connection with the Yamato kingdom. The tumulus has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] and its internal construction is unknown, but it is suspected to have a pit-type stone-lined [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus was surrounded by a moat, traces of which remain.
;Total length: 123 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 54 meters long x 51 meters wide x 6.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 72 meter diameter x 10.5 meters high, 2-tier
The tumulus is about 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kuragano Station|en|Kuragano Station|WD=Q910582|lt=Kuragano Station|1=Kuragano Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Takasaki Line|en|Takasaki Line|WD=Q1346657|lt=Takasaki Line|1=Takasaki Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The adjacent Kotsurumaki Kofun, which is roughly contemporary in age and similar in construction but on a smaller scale, is not covered by the National Historic Site designation.
